sect3.title: Overview

figure.title: Applying example for the Grid (legacy) filter

sect3.para: It renders a Cartesian grid on the active layer, on top of the existing contents. The width, spacing, offsets, and colors of the grid lines can all be set by the user. By default, the lines are black.

tip.para: By setting a grid line width to 0 you can disable showing that type of line (horizontal, vertical, intersection).

sect3.title: Activating the Filter
sect3.para:
This filter is found in the main menu under
Filters Render Pattern Grid (legacy)… .

sect3.title: Options
sect3.para: There are separate options for controlling horizontal grid lines, vertical grid lines, and intersections. By default, the horizontal and vertical settings are synchronized, so that all changes are applied symmetrically. If you want to change just one of them, click on the chain symbol below it to disable the synchronization.
For each of the first three sets of parameters you can select the unit of measurement for the values you enter. By default pixels is used.

listitem.para: Sets the thickness of the grid lines for horizontal, vertical and intersection lines respectively. By default, horizontal and vertical thickness is synchronized, which you can disable by toggling the chain symbol.
Sets the distance between grid lines. The Intersection parameter clears the space between the intersection point and the end of the arms of the intersection crosses.
Sets the starting offset for grid lines relative to the upper left corner. For intersections, sets the length of the arms of the intersection crosses.

The color selectors allow you to change the color of the horizontal, vertical and intersection grid lines separately. Click a color selector button to select a different color. By default, horizontal and vertical line colors are synchronized, which you can disable by toggling the chain symbol.
